intent: Define the SecurityRecipes approval receipt control for agentic AI: every human approval that unlocks a privileged agent action must be scope-bound, time-boxed, role-checked, separated from the requesting identity, linked to risk acceptance when needed, and sealed into a run receipt before the action is trusted.

positioning.category: Scope-bound approval receipts for agentic AI control planes
positioning.buyer: AI Platform, Security Engineering, IAM, GRC, product security, procurement security, and acquisition diligence teams
positioning.enterprise_promise: SecurityRecipes can prove that high-impact agent actions are not merely accompanied by a human approval string; they are backed by replayable approval receipts with approver roles, scope hashes, expiry, separation of duties, risk acceptance, and run evidence.
positioning.why_now: Agentic AI systems now chain MCP tools, browser actions, remote agents, persistent memory, model-provider routes, and non-human identities. Enterprises need a receipt primitive that turns human approval into enforceable policy instead of a chat transcript or pull-request comment.

standards_alignment.name: OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications 2026
standards_alignment.coverage: Maps approval receipts to tool misuse, identity and privilege abuse, cascading failures, human-agent trust exploitation, rogue-agent behavior, and unsafe action chains.

standards_alignment.name: OWASP Agentic Skills Top 10
standards_alignment.coverage: Requires approval receipts for behavior packages that request privileged permissions, shell or network execution, update authority, persistent memory, or cross-platform skill reuse.

standards_alignment.name: Model Context Protocol Authorization Specification
standards_alignment.coverage: Binds approval to protected-resource metadata, resource indicators, token audience validation, PKCE, short-lived tokens, and forbidden token passthrough before MCP authority expands.

standards_alignment.name: Model Context Protocol Security Best Practices
standards_alignment.coverage: Adds approval receipt checks to confused-deputy prevention, local MCP server command review, scope minimization, session binding, SSRF controls, and auditability.

standards_alignment.name: Model Context Protocol Elicitation Specification
standards_alignment.coverage: Uses elicitation as a receipt source only for non-sensitive approval rationale and requires URL-mode or external systems for sensitive authentication or payment flows.

standards_alignment.name: OpenAI Agents SDK Guardrails
standards_alignment.coverage: Aligns approval receipts with tool guardrails, tripwires, workflow boundaries, and safety-first execution modes before function tools perform side effects.

standards_alignment.name: NIST AI RMF Generative AI Profile
standards_alignment.coverage: Connects approval receipts to AI lifecycle governance, measurement, monitoring, data provenance, third-party risk, incident response, and risk treatment.

standards_alignment.name: CSA AI Controls Matrix
standards_alignment.coverage: Frames approval receipts as vendor-neutral control evidence that maps to AI accountability, lifecycle operations, access governance, monitoring, and enterprise assurance.

decisions.meaning: The approval is registered for the workflow and action class, scope-bound, unexpired, role-complete, separated from the requester, and linked to receipt evidence.
The approval is plausible but lacks the required approver count, required role mix, or separation-of-duties evidence.
The action profile requires explicit risk acceptance before execution.
The approved scope does not match the requested action envelope, workflow, tenant, run, identity, namespaces, or changed-path hash.
The approval is missing, pending, expired, malformed, issued by an untrusted source, or reused outside its allowed window.
The workflow and action class do not map to a registered approval profile.
The runtime observed approval bypass, post-execution approval, requester self-approval, secret transit, token passthrough, cross-tenant reuse, or a declared kill signal.

approval_profiles.title: Bounded remediation review
approval_profiles.commercial_value: Makes normal remediation PR approval machine-readable without adding unnecessary friction.

approval_profiles.title: Privileged tool or behavior-package step-up
approval_profiles.commercial_value: Creates a hosted approval-check surface for new skills, remote agents, memory writes, and privileged MCP tools.

approval_profiles.title: Identity and MCP scope change
approval_profiles.commercial_value: Turns non-human identity expansion into a reviewable, revocable, evidence-bound event.

approval_profiles.title: Production deployment or release approval
approval_profiles.commercial_value: Lets a production MCP gateway prove release authority before an agent changes live systems.

approval_profiles.title: Secret, regulated data, and egress boundary approval
approval_profiles.commercial_value: Gives regulated buyers a deterministic hold point before context, secrets, or tenant data can cross a boundary.

approval_profiles.title: Irreversible transaction approval
approval_profiles.commercial_value: Keeps high-consequence financial, blockchain, and irreversible workflows read-only until explicit two-key approval exists.

approval_profiles.title: Artifact quarantine and incident response approval
approval_profiles.commercial_value: Makes destructive quarantine actions inspectable and replayable for incident response and insurance-grade evidence.

buyer_views.title: AI Platform Runtime Gate
buyer_views.question: Can the gateway verify that a privileged agent action has a valid approval before execution?
buyer_views.answer_contract: Return approval profile, approver requirements, TTL, scope hash requirements, risk acceptance state, source artifacts, and the runtime decision.

buyer_views.title: IAM and GRC Review
buyer_views.question: Do non-human identities have separation-of-duties and access-review evidence before authority expands?
buyer_views.answer_contract: Return identity, workflow, action class, required roles, actual approver roles, risk acceptance, receipt id, and reviewer separation state.

buyer_views.title: Acquisition Diligence
buyer_views.question: Does SecurityRecipes have a credible hosted approval receipt product path?
buyer_views.answer_contract: Return open pack evidence, hosted validation path, integration surfaces, premium buyer value, and residual risk language.

executive_readout.board_level_claim: SecurityRecipes can convert human approval from a loose comment into a replayable control artifact that agent hosts can enforce before high-impact actions execute.
executive_readout.recommended_first_use: Attach approval receipt validation to action runtime gates, MCP gateway step-up prompts, entitlement review, and trust-center exports.
executive_readout.sales_motion: Lead with open approval receipt policy, then sell hosted receipt validation, approval-system connectors, two-key enforcement, risk acceptance workflows, and trust-center exports.

commercialization_path.open_layer: Public approval profiles, deterministic generator, runtime evaluator, generated JSON evidence pack, and docs for local MCP use.
commercialization_path.team_layer: Customer-specific approval profiles, source-host review adapters, change-management integration, and risk acceptance templates.
commercialization_path.enterprise_layer: Hosted approval receipt validation, SSO and ITSM connectors, two-key approval enforcement, receipt signing, SIEM export, and audit retention APIs.
commercialization_path.acquirer_value: A frontier lab, agent platform, or security vendor gets a missing control-plane primitive for making human authorization enforceable across MCP, A2A, browser agents, and autonomous action runtimes.
